questions.text: An inertial frame of reference is one which satisfies:

options.explanation: An inertial frame of reference is one that satisfies the following conditions: It is not accelerating. It is not rotating. It is not experiencing any external forces. In an inertial frame of reference, Newton's laws of motion hold true. This means that if an object is at rest in an inertial frame of reference, it will remain at rest unless acted upon by a force. Similarly, if an object is moving at a constant velocity in an inertial frame of reference, it will continue to move at that velocity unless acted upon by a force.

It was developed by Albert Einstein in 1905, and it is based on two postulates: The laws of physics are the same in all inertial frames of reference (that is, frames of reference with no acceleration). questions.text: Work done due to centripetal force for circular motion will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Reduced: The work done is a constant value of zero, not a reduced amount of work.
The value of work done is zero, not half of any value.
The work done is minimum (zero), not maximum. Maximum work would occur if the force and displacement were in the same direction.

The work done by the centripetal force in circular motion is not "reduced" because the centripetal force doesn't result in work in the first place. This is because the centripetal force acts perpendicular to the direction of motion, and work is defined as the product of force and displacement in the direction of the force. When you calculate the work done, you need to consider the component of the force that is in the direction of motion (parallel to the displacement). In the case of the centripetal force, there is no component of the force in the direction of motion, as it always acts perpendicular to the displacement. In mathematical terms, when you calculate work as W = F * d * cos(θ), the θ (angle) between the force and displacement vectors is 90 degrees (perpendicular), and the cosine of 90 degrees is 0. Therefore, the work done by the centripetal force is zero, not half or any other fraction. So, in the context of circular motion, the centripetal force does not perform any work on the object because the force and the direction of motion are always perpendicular.

questions.explanation: The work done due to the centripetal force in circular motion is zero. This is because the centripetal force acts perpendicular to the displacement of the object in its circular path. In other words, the force is always directed toward the centre of the circle, while the displacement is along the circumference of the circle.The work done (W) is defined as the dot product of force (F) and displacement (d):W = F * d * cos(θ)Where:W is the work done.F is the force applied.d is the displacement.θ is the angle between the force and the displacement vectors.In the case of centripetal force in circular motion, the angle θ between the force and displacement vectors is 90 degrees (perpendicular), so the cosine of 90 degrees is 0. Therefore, the work done is:W = F * d * cos(90) = F * d * 0 = 0This means that no work is done by the centripetal force in circular motion because the force is always perpendicular to the direction of motion.

questions.text: Which inert gas is mixed with oxygen gas by deep-sea divers to adjust the partial pressure of oxygen gas?

questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: False!A mix of helium and oxygen is often used by deep sea divers for breathing because it reduces the risk of developing decompression sickness, also known as "the bends." The bends can occur when a diver ascends too quickly from a deep dive, causing nitrogen bubbles to form in their bloodstream and tissues. These bubbles can cause joint pain, fatigue, and even neurological damage in severe cases. Helium, which has a lower molecular weight than nitrogen, is less soluble in blood and tissues and therefore reduces the risk of bubble formation during ascent. Oxygen, on the other hand, is essential for sustaining life and provides the necessary gas for cellular respiration. However, high levels of oxygen can be toxic to the body and can cause seizures and other adverse effects. Therefore, a mixture of helium and oxygen is used to balance the benefits of reduced nitrogen exposure with the need for a sufficient oxygen supply.

questions.text: A material whose K absorption edge is 0.15 Å is irradiated with 0.1 Å X-rays. The maximum kinetic energy of the photoelectrons that are emitted from the K-shell is :
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Correct. This is the calculated maximum kinetic energy of the photoelectrons using the difference in energy between the incident X-rays and the K absorption edge.To determine the maximum kinetic energy of the photoelectrons, we need to calculate the energy of the incident X-rays and the energy corresponding to the K absorption edge. The energy of a photon is given by E = hc/λ, where h is Planck's constant and c is the speed of light.The energy of the 0.1 Å X-rays is calculated as:Eincident = (6.626 x 10-34 J·s)(3 x 108 m/s) / (0.1 x 10-10 m) = 1.986 x 10-15 J = 124 keVThe energy corresponding to the K absorption edge is:EK = (6.626 x 10-34 J·s)(3 x 108 m/s) / (0.15 x 10-10 m) = 1.324 x 10-15 J = 83 keVThe maximum kinetic energy of the photoelectrons is the difference:Ekinetic = Eincident - EK = 124 keV - 83 keV = 41 keVThus, the correct answer is 41 keV. The other options represent incorrect calculations of this energy difference, leading to overestimated or inconsistent values.
